summary: Iteration 8 — deploy-blocker (minimal requirements.txt) verified end-to-end. Backend restarted cleanly with the slimmed dependency set, all critical API endpoints respond 200, pip resolution in a fresh venv is conflict-free, server.py imports map 1:1 onto the new requirements, and the frontend smoke (login + sidebar) works.

static_check_requirements_txt.result: PASS — exactly the 12 required packages, no emergentintegrations / litellm / boto3 / black / ruff / pytest / pyflakes

import_audit_server_py.notes: starlette ships with fastapi; jwt = PyJWT; dotenv = python-dotenv; Pillow is a transitive dep needed by qrcode[pil]; python-multipart is needed for fastapi UploadFile/File/Form which server.py imports. No orphan imports.

pip_resolution_dry_run.result: PASS — resolver picked motor 3.7.1 + pymongo 4.17.0 with no conflict, all 12 top-level + transitive deps resolved cleanly
pip_resolution_dry_run.resolved_versions: fastapi-0.115.14, uvicorn-0.33.0, motor-3.7.1, pymongo-4.17.0, pydantic-2.13.4, python-dotenv-1.2.2, bcrypt-4.3.0, PyJWT-2.13.0, qrcode-8.2, pillow-11.3.0, httpx-0.28.1, slowapi-0.1.10, python-multipart-0.0.32, starlette-0.46.2

backend_runtime_verification.supervisor_status: backend RUNNING (uptime ~43s at test time — fresh restart with new requirements)

frontend_smoke.console_errors: Only pre-existing Recharts width(-1) warnings during initial render (not regression, not blocking)

critical_code_review_comments: requirements.txt is well-commented, version-ranged (>=,<), and lean — good hygiene for the VPS docker build.

python-multipart and Pillow are correctly included as direct deps even though they are technically transitive (UploadFile/File/Form import + qrcode[pil]) — this makes the deps explicit and resolver-stable. Keep them.

server.py is now 1115 lines — approaching the 700-line guideline. Not blocking, but consider splitting routers (auth, sessions, messages, admin, public-api, webhook) into separate modules in a future iteration for maintainability.
No regressions introduced. The minimal requirements set is sufficient to run the entire app.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 8 only swapped /app/backend/requirements.txt to a 12-package minimal list (drops emergentintegrations + 50+ unused deps). Verified at runtime: supervisor restart healthy, all critical APIs 200, frontend login + sidebar work. Fresh-venv pip --dry-run resolves with no conflict. User's docker build on VPS should now succeed. No app code changed.
rca_of_the_issue: Original requirements.txt was a `pip freeze` dump including emergentintegrations==0.2.0 (Emergent-internal-only PyPI package). On the user's VPS this broke `docker compose build` with `Could not find a version that satisfies the requirement emergentintegrations==0.2.0`. Fix: hand-curated minimal requirements.txt with version-range pins so motor/pymongo/pydantic stay compatible. Verified runtime + clean-venv resolution.
